auto_posts.title: Azure Local Update — February 5, 2026
auto_posts.subtitle: Documentation changes
auto_posts.content:
- Applies to upgrades from Azure Stack HCI 22H2 (out of support) to 23H2 or 24H2, and from 23H2 to 24H2. The LCM account must have explicit "Log on as a batch job" rights—a permission that is not automatically inherited from local Administrator group membership alone. → Solution upgrade prerequisites

- Operations teams upgrading from 22H2 (now out of support) need to configure this Active Directory right before running the solution upgrade, as the prerequisite validation now checks for this permission during the upgrade readiness phase.

- The "Log on as a batch job" policy must be added through Group Policy Editor (gppedit.msc) or Active Directory Group Policy: navigate to Computer Configuration > Windows Settings > Security Settings > Local Policies > User Rights Assignment.

- This requirement affects solution upgrades initiated via the Azure portal's upgrade workflow. Teams should audit existing LCM accounts before beginning the upgrade process to avoid validation failures.

- Missing this permission may cause the solution upgrade prerequisites validation to fail with authentication-related errors; verify compliance before initiating the upgrade from your solution upgrade readiness check.

Related documentation: Install solution upgrade on Azure Local
